subject: Product Management
title: How Amazon Discovers
description: A micro-lesson that makes How Amazon Discovers usable.

hook.text: At Amazon, every new product idea starts with a fake press release — written before a single line of code exists.

buildup.text: The 'working backwards' method forces teams to articulate the customer benefit first and the technology second.

discovery.text: If the press release doesn't excite you, the product won't either. Bad press releases kill bad ideas early.

twist.text: Amazon also bans PowerPoint in meetings. Instead, teams write six-page narratives that force rigorous thinking.

climax.text: Working backwards has produced Kindle, AWS, Prime, and Alexa — all started as press releases before prototypes.

punchline.text: Write the press release first. Build the product second.

quiz.question: What is Amazon's 'working backwards' discovery method?
options: Writing a fake press release to articulate customer benefit before building
Starting with the technology and finding a use for it
